content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1–4.5.3 WARN — push entitlement (App/DealAlerts.entitlements: aps-environment) combined with hourly repeating promo notifications (App/NotificationScheduler.swift:12, `timeInterval: 3600, repeats: true`) and metadata promising "EVERY HOUR … unlimited notifications" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:3) may trigger review questions about notification spam.
Pierre: Apple's 4.5.3/4.5.4 rules prohibit using notifications for spam or unsolicited marketing not requested by the user, and both the code and the store description explicitly advertise hourly promotional pushes all day long. This pattern reads as marketing spam rather than user-initiated alerts, and reviewers often flag it directly from the description text alone. Consider making deal alerts opt-in per user preference (frequency controls, marketing-notification consent) and rewording the description to drop the "every hour / unlimited" promise.
